unit.comment: /*************************************************************************** * _ _ ____ _ * Project ___| | | | _ \| | * / __| | | | |_) | | * | (__| |_| | _ <| |___ * \___|\___/|_| \_\_____| * * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al. * * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html. * * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file. * * This software is distributed on an "AS IS" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY * KIND, either express or implied. * * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl * * RFC1734 POP3 Authentication * RFC1939 POP3 protocol * RFC2195 CRAM-MD5 authentication * RFC2384 POP URL Scheme * RFC2449 POP3 Extension Mechanism * RFC2595 Using TLS with IMAP, POP3 and ACAP * RFC2831 DIGEST-MD5 authentication * RFC4422 Simple Authentication and Security Layer (SASL) * RFC4616 PLAIN authentication * RFC4752 The Kerberos V5 ("GSSAPI") SASL Mechanism * RFC5034 POP3 SASL Authentication Mechanism * RFC6749 OAuth 2.0 Authorization Framework * RFC8314 Use of TLS for Email Submission and Access * Draft LOGIN SASL Mechanism <draft-murchison-sasl-login-00.txt> * ***************************************************************************/
/* The last 3 #include files should be in this order */
unit.function_decl: static CURLcode pop3_done(struct Curl_easy *data, CURLcode status, bool premature);
static CURLcode pop3_disconnect(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, bool dead);
static int pop3_getsock(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, curl_socket_t *socks);
static CURLcode pop3_setup_connection(struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn);
static CURLcode pop3_perform_auth(struct Curl_easy *data, const char *mech, const struct bufref *initresp);
static CURLcode pop3_continue_auth(struct Curl_easy *data, const char *mech, const struct bufref *resp);
static CURLcode pop3_cancel_auth(struct Curl_easy *data, const char *mech);
static CURLcode pop3_get_message(struct Curl_easy *data, struct bufref *out);
unit.decl_stmt: const struct Curl_handler Curl_handler_pop3 = { "POP3", /* scheme */ pop3_setup_connection, /* setup_connection */ pop3_do, /* do_it */ pop3_done, /* done */ ZERO_NULL, /* do_more */ pop3_connect, /* connect_it */ pop3_multi_statemach, /* connecting */ pop3_doing, /* doing */ pop3_getsock, /* proto_getsock */ pop3_getsock, /* doing_getsock */ ZERO_NULL, /* domore_getsock */ ZERO_NULL, /* perform_getsock */ pop3_disconnect, /* disconnect */ ZERO_NULL, /* write_resp */ ZERO_NULL, /* connection_check */ ZERO_NULL, /* attach connection */ PORT_POP3, /* defport */ CURLPROTO_POP3, /* protocol */ CURLPROTO_POP3, /* family */ PROTOPT_CLOSEACTION | PROTOPT_NOURLQUERY | /* flags */ PROTOPT_URLOPTIONS };

const struct Curl_handler Curl_handler_pop3s = { "POP3S", /* scheme */ pop3_setup_connection, /* setup_connection */ pop3_do, /* do_it */ pop3_done, /* done */ ZERO_NULL, /* do_more */ pop3_connect, /* connect_it */ pop3_multi_statemach, /* connecting */ pop3_doing, /* doing */ pop3_getsock, /* proto_getsock */ pop3_getsock, /* doing_getsock */ ZERO_NULL, /* domore_getsock */ ZERO_NULL, /* perform_getsock */ pop3_disconnect, /* disconnect */ ZERO_NULL, /* write_resp */ ZERO_NULL, /* connection_check */ ZERO_NULL, /* attach connection */ PORT_POP3S, /* defport */ CURLPROTO_POP3S, /* protocol */ CURLPROTO_POP3, /* family */ PROTOPT_CLOSEACTION | PROTOPT_SSL | PROTOPT_NOURLQUERY | PROTOPT_URLOPTIONS /* flags */ };
unit.comment: /* SASL parameters for the pop3 protocol */
unit.decl_stmt: static const struct SASLproto saslpop3 = { "pop", /* The service name */ pop3_perform_auth, /* Send authentication command */ pop3_continue_auth, /* Send authentication continuation */ pop3_cancel_auth, /* Send authentication cancellation */ pop3_get_message, /* Get SASL response message */ 255 - 8, /* Max line len - strlen("AUTH ") - 1 space - crlf */ '*', /* Code received when continuation is expected */ '+', /* Code to receive upon authentication success */ SASL_AUTH_DEFAULT, /* Default mechanisms */ SASL_FLAG_BASE64 /* Configuration flags */ };

function.name: pop3_to_pop3s

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * pop3_endofresp() * * Checks for an ending POP3 status code at the start of the given string, but * also detects the APOP timestamp from the server greeting and various * capabilities from the CAPA response including the supported authentication * types and allowed SASL mechanisms. */

function.name: pop3_endofresp
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, char *line, size_t len, int *resp)

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * pop3_get_message() * * Gets the authentication message from the response buffer. */

function.name: pop3_get_message
pop3_state

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * pop3_perform_capa() * * Sends the CAPA command in order to obtain a list of server side supported * capabilities. */

function.name: pop3_perform_capa

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * pop3_perform_starttls() * * Sends the STLS command to start the upgrade to TLS. */

function.name: pop3_perform_starttls
pop3_perform_upgrade_tls

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * pop3_perform_user() * * Sends a clear text USER command to authenticate with. */

function.name: pop3_perform_user
pop3_perform_apop

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * pop3_perform_auth() * * Sends an AUTH command allowing the client to login with the given SASL * authentication mechanism. */

function.name: pop3_perform_auth
pop3_continue_auth
pop3_cancel_auth

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * pop3_perform_authentication() * * Initiates the authentication sequence, with the appropriate SASL * authentication mechanism, falling back to APOP and clear text should a * common mechanism not be available between the client and server. */

function.name: pop3_perform_authentication
pop3_perform_command
pop3_perform_quit
pop3_state_servergreet_resp
pop3_state_capa_resp
pop3_state_starttls_resp
function.parameter_list: (struct Curl_easy *data, struct connectdata *conn, int pop3code, pop3state instate)

function.name: pop3_state_auth_resp
pop3_state_apop_resp
pop3_state_user_resp
pop3_state_pass_resp
pop3_state_command_resp
pop3_statemachine

unit.comment: /* Called repeatedly until done from multi.c */

function.name: pop3_multi_statemach
pop3_block_statemach

unit.comment: /* Allocate and initialize the POP3 struct for the current Curl_easy if required */

function.name: pop3_init

unit.comment: /* For the POP3 "protocol connect" and "doing" phases only */

function.name: pop3_getsock

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * pop3_connect() * * This function should do everything that is to be considered a part of the * connection phase. * * The variable 'done' points to will be TRUE if the protocol-layer connect * phase is done when this function returns, or FALSE if not. */

function.name: pop3_connect

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * pop3_done() * * The DONE function. This does what needs to be done after a single DO has * performed. * * Input argument is already checked for validity. */

function.name: pop3_done

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * pop3_perform() * * This is the actual DO function for POP3. Get a message/listing according to * the options previously setup. */

function.name: pop3_perform

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * pop3_do() * * This function is registered as 'curl_do' function. It decodes the path * parts etc as a wrapper to the actual DO function (pop3_perform). * * The input argument is already checked for validity. */

function.name: pop3_do

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * pop3_disconnect() * * Disconnect from an POP3 server. Cleanup protocol-specific per-connection * resources. BLOCKING. */

function.name: pop3_disconnect

unit.comment: /* Call this when the DO phase has completed */

function.name: pop3_dophase_done
pop3_doing

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * pop3_regular_transfer() * * The input argument is already checked for validity. * * Performs all commands done before a regular transfer between a local and a * remote host. */

function.name: pop3_regular_transfer
pop3_setup_connection
pop3_parse_url_options
pop3_parse_url_path
pop3_parse_custom_request

unit.comment: /*********************************************************************** * * Curl_pop3_write() * * This function scans the body after the end-of-body and writes everything * until the end is found. */

function.name: Curl_pop3_write
